function.name: visualise_data
function.description: Visualise financial data as a chart. Supports spending, income, and cash flow. Can group by category, payee, or month, and compare across two time periods. The frontend determines the best chart type based on the data.

last_n_months.description: Query the last N complete calendar months (excluding current month). e.g. last_n_months: 1 = last month, last_n_months: 6 = last 6 months.

from_date.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Use for the current month (1st of this month), current year (Jan 1st), or any specific date range. Includes up to today unless to_date is set.

to_date.description: End date (YYYY-MM-DD), inclusive. Defaults to today if omitted. Only use with from_date.

last_n_months.description: Query the last N complete calendar months (excluding current month). e.g. last_n_months: 1 = last month, last_n_months: 6 = last 6 months.

from_date.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Use for the current month (1st of this month), current year (Jan 1st), or any specific date range. Includes up to today unless to_date is set.

to_date.description: End date (YYYY-MM-DD), inclusive. Defaults to today if omitted. Only use with from_date.

group_by.description: How to group/break down the data. Use 'category' or 'payee' for entity breakdowns, 'month' for time series. Combine e.g. ['category', 'month'] for a monthly breakdown by category.

filter.description: Narrow to specific categories or payees. Does not break down the data — use group_by for that.

chart_title.description: Short title summarising what the chart shows. Always generate one — never ask the user.
